folio.label: 85v
folio.l: Et incipit ¸logus Su~monitoris
THis Sompnoå , in hise stiropes heye stode
Vp on this frere , his hert was so wode
That like an Aspen lef , he quok for ire
Lordynggis q¶ he , but o thyngö I desire
I ©ow byseche , that of ©oure curteisye
Syn ©e han herd , this fals frere lye
As suffretú me , I may my tale telle
This frere bostetú , that he knowetú helle
And god it woot , that is but litel wonder
Freres and fendes , ben but lite a sonder
For ¹de , ©e han ofte tyme herd telle
How that a frere , rauysshed was to helle
In Spirit oones , by a vysiouö
And as an Aungel , led hi~ vp and doun
To shewen hi~ , the peynes that there were
In al that place , saw he nat a frere
Of othere folk , he saw I nowe in woo
Vn to this Aungel , spak this frere thoo
¢ Now sire q¶ he , han freres swicú a grace
That noone of he~ , shal comen in this place
ñis q¶ this Aungel , many a mylioun~
And vn to Sathanas , he led hi~ doun
And now hatú Sathanas , seitú he , a tayle
Braddere than of a Caryk , is the Sayle
Hold vp thy tayl , thow Sathanas q¶ he
Shewe fortú thyn Ars , and late the frere se
Where is the nest , of freres in this place
And er that half a furlong weye , of space
Rigút so as Bees , out swarmen of an hyue
Out of Deueles Ars , they gonne dryue
Twenty thousand freres , on a route
And thurgú out helle , they swarmed al a boute
And comen a geyn , as fast as they moun~ gou~
And in his Ars , they crepten euäychou~
He clapte his tayl a geyn , & lay ful stille
This frere , whan he loked had , his fille
Vp on the turmentes , of this sory place
